top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The day's dominant thread is great-power choreography: Putin landed in Beijing for a summit with Xi just days after Trump left, with the two sides extending their 25-year friendship pact and lining up roughly 40 energy and trade deals. In parallel, Trump pulled back from an Iran strike he said was 'an hour away', citing Gulf-state pleas for more time on a deal - while the UAE traced the Barakah-plant drones to Iraqi territory, pointing the finger at Iran-backed militias. Oil sits near $105 with Hormuz still shut, the Ebola PHEIC continues to expand in DRC and Uganda, and Bolivia's capital is under siege amid mass anti-government protests.

stories.headline: Xi and Putin meet in Beijing, extend 25-year friendship treaty days after Trump's visit
stories.summary: Xi Jinping welcomed Vladimir Putin at the Great Hall of the People on May 20, with the two leaders extending the 2001 Treaty of Good-Neighbourliness and warning against a global return to 'the law of the jungle'. Some 40 deals are expected, dominated by energy - including the long-stalled Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline. The summit comes a week after Trump's own Beijing trip, underscoring China's pivotal role between the two powers.

stories.headline: Trump says he was 'an hour away' from striking Iran before postponing at Gulf states' request
stories.summary: President Trump said he scrapped a planned May 19 strike on Iran after Tehran passed a peace proposal via intermediaries and Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the UAE pleaded for more time. He told reporters Iran has 'two or three days, maybe until Sunday' to come to terms, and ordered the Pentagon to stay ready for a 'full, large-scale assault' if no deal is reached.

stories.headline: UAE traces Barakah-plant drones to Iraqi territory; six more intercepted in 48 hours
stories.summary: The UAE Defence Ministry said technical tracking confirmed all three drones that struck the Barakah nuclear plant on May 17 launched from Iraq - and that six additional Iraq-origin drones were intercepted in the next two days. Authorities stopped short of formally blaming Iran, but the trajectory points at Iran-backed Shiite militias. The four Barakah reactors are still operating normally.

stories.headline: Oil holds near $105 as Hormuz stays shut and inventories near record lows
stories.summary: Brent settled around $105 and US crude near $100 even after Trump postponed the strike. The IEA warns oil inventories are depleting at a 'record pace' with Hormuz - which moves about 20% of global oil trade - still effectively closed since late February. UBS expects stockpiles to test all-time lows by the end of May.

stories.headline: Bolivia's capital under siege as anti-Paz protests, blockades enter third week
stories.summary: Less than six months into President Rodrigo Paz's term, La Paz is paralysed by miner-led blockades that have emptied markets and drained hospital oxygen supplies. Protests now demand his resignation, with at least three deaths blamed on blocked emergency vehicles. Paz annulled the land-mortgage law that triggered the unrest, but Bolivia's 14% inflation and dollar shortage have widened the revolt.

stories.headline: WHO 'very concerned' as Ebola epidemic spreads to capitals of DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: The WHO Director-General said he is 'very concerned' about how fast the Bundibugyo-strain epidemic is moving. 8 confirmed and 246 suspected cases with 80 suspected deaths are concentrated in Ituri Province, but confirmed cases are now in Kinshasa and Kampala. No vaccine exists for this strain and case-fatality runs to 50%.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia trade biggest drone barrages of the war
stories.summary: Russia said it shot down four more drones headed for Moscow on May 19 after a record May 16-17 wave in which over 550 were downed. Ukrainian SBU strikes hit an oil refinery and two pumping stations in the Moscow region. Russia retaliated by damaging Ukraine's Danube-river grain-export port of Izmail. Zelenskyy called the home-front strikes 'entirely justified'.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Inter-monsoon weather dominates daily life - NEA expects thundery showers most afternoons through end-May with highs of 33-35°C. The big public-safety story remains the deepfake scam in which a victim wired S$4.9 million after a fake Zoom call with PM Wong; SPF has now released the AI-generated footage. CDA confirms both Singapore residents flagged from the MV Hondius hantavirus cruise tested negative. PM Wong spoke at EMA's 25th-anniversary dinner on May 19, while SM Lee Hsien Loong is in Guangxi and Shanghai through May 22.

stories.headline: Police release deepfake Zoom footage from S$4.9m PM Wong impersonation scam
stories.summary: Singapore Police have now released video of an AI-generated Zoom conference in which a deepfake of PM Lawrence Wong, President Tharman, Minister Indranee Rajah and 'MAS officials' persuaded a businessman to wire S$4.9 million for purported Strait of Hormuz funding. Victims were first contacted on WhatsApp by a scammer claiming to be the cabinet secretary. SPF is urging the public to verify any ministerial outreach through official channels.

stories.headline: PM Wong addresses Energy Market Authority's 25th anniversary gala
stories.summary: PM and Finance Minister Lawrence Wong spoke at the Energy Market Authority's 25th-anniversary gala on May 19, underscoring Singapore's push for a clean and resilient energy system as regional gas markets stay volatile and Hormuz remains shut. The speech is part of a broader government effort to reassure on energy security.

stories.headline: SM Lee Hsien Loong in Guangxi and Shanghai through May 22
stories.summary: Senior Minister Lee Hsien Loong is on an official visit to Guangxi and Shanghai from May 18 to 22 to deepen Singapore-China cooperation, including on logistics, fintech and green-economy projects under the Suzhou Industrial Park and Tianjin Eco-City frameworks.

stories.headline: CDA: Both Singapore residents flagged from MV Hondius test negative for hantavirus
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency confirms the two Singapore residents who had been on the Andes-hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius have tested negative after isolation at NCID. There is no community transmission and the public-health risk remains low.

stories.headline: NEA: Thundery showers expected most afternoons through end of May
stories.summary: Inter-monsoon conditions persist. NEA forecasts short, gusty thundery showers most afternoons - occasionally extending into the evening - with Sumatra-squall pre-dawn rain on a few days. Daily highs of 33-34°C are expected, touching 35°C on hotter days.

stories.headline: May COE round 1: Cat A $124,790, Cat B $126,236, Cat E $127,700
stories.summary: LTA's May 2026 first-bidding exercise closed with Cat A (cars up to 1600cc) at $124,790, Cat B (larger cars) at $126,236, Cat C (goods vehicles & buses) at $87,479, Cat D (motorcycles) at $9,452 and Cat E (open) at $127,700. The May-July quota is 19,052 COEs - 1% above the prior quarter - but premiums remain near record highs.

stories.headline: HDB: June 2026 BTO application deadline closed May 15; ~19,600 flats this year
stories.summary: Applications for the June 2026 BTO exercise closed on May 15, the second of three sales exercises planned for 2026 (Feb, Jun, Oct). HDB will launch about 19,600 flats this year, including more than 4,000 Shorter Waiting Time (SWT) units with sub-three-year wait times. The 'third-child' allocation has doubled from 5% to 10%.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three active theatres are trending the wrong way again. Ukraine and Russia traded record drone barrages this week and Russia hit a Ukrainian Danube grain port in retaliation. The US-Iran ceasefire is technically holding but the Strait of Hormuz remains shut, and the UAE has now traced the Barakah drone strike to Iraq, pointing at Iran-backed militias. Israel's truces in Lebanon and Gaza are fraying: an Israeli strike killed 10 in south Lebanon on May 19 and a senior US envoy warns Gaza is heading toward a permanent partition with Israel controlling ~64% of the enclave.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active - Day 1543 of full-scale invasion; record drone exchanges this week

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine
Front lines harden across Donbas; Ukraine cross-border incursion into Kursk region
Stalemate continues; Russia gains roughly 1,714 sq mi (0.7% of Ukraine) over the year to May 2026
Russia launches a record 8,000+ drones in a single month - the highest since the war began
Net analysis: Russia loses 45 sq mi between Apr 14 - May 12 as Ukrainian counter-attacks push back
Russia fires over 1,560 drones at Ukraine in two days - the biggest aerial assault of the war; Kyiv strikes kill at least 25
Ukraine launches its largest counter-barrage; Russia says it intercepted 556 drones across 14 regions; an oil refinery and two pumping stations near Moscow are hit, 4 killed
Russia downs four more drones headed for Moscow; Russian strikes damage Ukraine's Danube-river grain-export port of Izmail in retaliation
Combat continues at high intensity; Putin in Beijing for a summit with Xi - the optics of a 'no-limits partner' play directly into the war narrative

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran Conflict (incl. Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Ceasefire technically in effect; Hormuz still shut; new Barakah-drone escalation traced to Iraq

timeline.event: US and Israel launch coordinated strikes on Iran under Operation Epic Fury; Supreme Leader Khamenei killed
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'closed'; tanker traffic collapses, 20,000+ mariners eventually stranded in the Gulf
US-Iran ceasefire signed, including Israel; offensive phase declared over
IMO reports ~2,000 ships still stranded outside the Strait
Iran intercepts three missiles (a fourth falls into the sea) - the first reported incident since the ceasefire
Drone strike sparks fire at the perimeter of UAE's Barakah nuclear plant; UAE opens an investigation
Trump postpones a 'scheduled attack' on Iran planned for May 19 at the request of Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the UAE
Trump says he was 'an hour away' from striking Iran; UAE confirms Barakah-strike drones came from Iraq; 6 more Iraq-origin drones intercepted in 48h
Hormuz oil flow stays near 30% of pre-war levels; Brent around $105 as the diplomatic window stays open through 'Sunday or early next week'

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon/Gaza
theatres.status: Ceasefires fraying; Gaza approaching permanent partition

timeline.event: Hamas attack triggers war in Gaza
Israel expands operations into southern Lebanon against Hezbollah
Renewed Israel-Hezbollah war begins; Israel invades parts of Lebanon
Senior US ceasefire envoy warns Gaza risks being permanently divided; Israel now controls ~64% of the enclave (the 'orange line')
Lebanon truce extended by another 45 days but fighting continues; Israeli strike in Gaza City kills Hamas-in-Gaza leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad
Israeli strike on southern Lebanon kills at least 10, including three women and three children
Cumulative toll in Lebanon now exceeds 3,000 dead and 1 million displaced (~20% of Lebanon's population); near-daily Israeli strikes in Gaza have killed 850+ since the truce began

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius Andes-hantavirus cluster has stabilised at 11 cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable) per ECDC's May 19 update, with 3 reported deaths. The Dutch-flagged ship has docked in Rotterdam with 25 crew and 2 medics on board after passenger repatriation. Both Singapore residents flagged from the voyage have tested negative. WHO still assesses epidemic risk as low - Andes virus is the only hantavirus known to spread human-to-human, but historic spread has been confined to close-contact settings.

stories.headline: ECDC May 19 update: 11 hantavirus cases linked to MV Hondius, 3 deaths
stories.summary: ECDC's latest count is 11 cases - 9 laboratory-confirmed and 2 probable - tied to the cruise ship, with 3 deaths. Passengers came from 23 countries, nine of them EU/EEA. ECDC has issued rapid scientific advice on managing repatriated passengers.

stories.headline: MV Hondius docks in Rotterdam with 25 crew and 2 medical staff aboard
stories.summary: After passenger repatriation, the Dutch-flagged MV Hondius arrived in Rotterdam on May 18 with 25 crew and 2 medical professionals still on board. Disembarkation has begun and individuals are isolating; deep cleaning and disinfection of the ship are under way.

stories.headline: Singapore CDA: Both flagged residents test negative; public risk remains low
stories.summary: The two Singapore residents who had been aboard MV Hondius have tested negative after isolation at NCID. CDA reiterates there is no evidence of community transmission and the public-health risk to Singapore remains low.

stories.headline: CDC: Andes virus is the only hantavirus known to spread person-to-person
stories.summary: Unlike most hantaviruses (rodent-borne and not human-to-human), Andes virus can spread through close, sustained contact and possibly aerosols. It causes hantavirus pulmonary syndrome with a 1-7 week incubation period and 20-40% case-fatality. No specific approved treatment exists; care is supportive.

stories.headline: WHO: Risk of epidemic remains low; previous outbreaks contained to close-contact settings
stories.summary: WHO continues to rate epidemic risk as low. Historic Andes-virus clusters have spread in family or healthcare-worker contact networks, not through casual community transmission. Contact-tracing and surveillance across affected countries is ongoing.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSec
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Two fresh stories dominate this evening: Drupal is shipping an emergency 'highly critical' core security release between 17:00-21:00 UTC tonight (PSA-2026-05-18) across all supported branches - all Drupal sites should reserve a maintenance window. And a compromised Nx Console VS Code extension (v18.95.0) - installed by 2.2M+ developers - was used in a supply-chain attack that exfiltrates GitHub, npm, AWS, Vault and 1Password secrets, and explicitly targets ~/.claude/settings.json. On top of that, Cisco SD-WAN's CVSS-10 auth-bypass is in CISA KEV with a federal deadline already passed, and a SEPPMail email-gateway CVSS-10 path-traversal exposes enterprise mail.

stories.headline: Drupal to ship emergency 'highly critical' core security release tonight 17:00-21:00 UTC
stories.summary: Drupal's security team has pre-announced a 'highly critical' (20/25, AC=none, AU=none) core release across all supported branches on May 20, 17:00-21:00 UTC. Even fully end-of-life Drupal 8/9 will get manual patch files. Drupal 11.x admins must be on 11.1.9+ and 10.x admins on 10.4.9+ to apply the patch cleanly. Exploits are expected within hours of the release.

stories.headline: Compromised Nx Console VS Code extension targets developer + cloud secrets - and Claude Code configs
stories.summary: A malicious version (18.95.0) of the Nx Console VS Code extension (2.2M+ installs) was published to the Marketplace on May 18 for ~11 minutes. It silently fetched a 498 KB payload from an orphan commit in the nrwl/nx repo that exfiltrates GitHub, npm, AWS, HashiCorp Vault, Kubernetes and 1Password tokens via HTTPS, GitHub API and DNS tunnels - and explicitly targets ~/.claude/settings.json. Update to 18.100.0+ and rotate every developer secret.

stories.headline: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN auth-bypass CVE-2026-20182 (CVSS 10) actively exploited
stories.summary: An unauthenticated remote attacker can fully bypass authentication and gain admin on Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controllers. CISA added it to the KEV on May 15 with a federal patch deadline of May 17 - meaning unpatched federal estates are now out of compliance. Active exploitation in the wild is confirmed.

stories.headline: SEPPMail Secure Email Gateway: path-traversal CVE-2026-2743 (CVSS 10) enables full mail-traffic read
stories.summary: A path-traversal flaw in SEPPMail's large file transfer (LFT) feature lets unauthenticated attackers write arbitrary files - leading to remote code execution and the ability to read all inbound and outbound mail. A second flaw (CVE-2026-7864, CVSS 6.9) leaks server environment variables via an unauthenticated GINA UI endpoint.

stories.headline: NGINX heap overflow CVE-2026-42945 exploited in the wild; worker crashes, possible RCE
stories.summary: A heap-buffer overflow in ngx_http_rewrite_module affects NGINX 0.6.27 through 1.30.0 - essentially everything in production today. Active exploitation is causing worker crashes; full RCE is plausible. Upgrade to the fixed branch immediately.

stories.headline: Apache HTTP/2 double-free CVE-2026-23918 can DoS and possibly RCE
stories.summary: A double-free in HTTP/2 handling affects Apache HTTP Server 2.4.66 and is fixed in 2.4.67. Most public exploitation so far is denial-of-service, but memory-corruption primitives raise the RCE risk.

stories.headline: Linux kernel LPE CVE-2026-31431 added to CISA KEV after in-the-wild use
stories.summary: A local-privilege-escalation flaw lets an unprivileged Linux user obtain root. CISA added it to the KEV after confirmed in-the-wild exploitation, requiring federal patches under the standard 21-day timeline.

stories.headline: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday: 120 fixes, no zero-days but 17 Critical RCEs
stories.summary: Microsoft's May Patch Tuesday addressed 120 vulnerabilities, 17 rated Critical (14 RCE), with no zero-days. Notable: CVE-2026-35421 (Windows GDI RCE via malicious EMF in Paint) and CVE-2026-41096 (Windows DNS Client RCE via crafted DNS response). Office preview-pane RCEs make rapid rollout important.

stories.headline: Grafana Labs: breach contained to GitHub environment; no production impact
stories.summary: Grafana Labs said on May 19 that its investigation into the Coinbase Cartel ransomware-group intrusion found no evidence customer production systems or operations were compromised. The scope is limited to public and private source code in Grafana's GitHub environment.

stories.headline: ShinyHunters claim 275M education records stolen from Canvas/Instructure platform
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters group claims to have exfiltrated about 275 million records belonging to students, teachers and staff across 8,809 school districts, universities and online-education platforms. Per-institution counts range from tens of thousands to several million. Confirmations and notifications are still rolling out.
